title: arbulocarpus somalensis
intro: Arbulocarpus somalensis, also known as the Somali birch or the Somali paper bark tree, is a deciduous tree species native to the Horn of Africa. This tree has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, and is valued for its ability to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Arbulocarpus somalensis include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating digestive problems. The herbal preparations of this tree typically involve the bark and leaves, which can be made into a decoction or infusions for medicinal use.
